questions.question: Two statements about diamond are given. 1   Diamond has a giant three-dimensional covalent structure of carbon atoms. 2   Diamond is one of the hardest substances known. Which is correct? A Both statements are correct and statement 1 explains statement 2., B Both statements are correct but statement 2 does not explain statement 1., C Statement 1 is correct but statement 2 is incorrect., D Statement 2 is correct but statement 1 is incorrect. 3
questions.text: Two statements about diamond are given. 1   Diamond has a giant three-dimensional covalent structure of carbon atoms. 2   Diamond is one of the hardest substances known. Which is correct? A Both statements are correct and statement 1 explains statement 2., B Both statements are correct but statement 2 does not explain statement 1., C Statement 1 is correct but statement 2 is incorrect., D Statement 2 is correct but statement 1 is incorrect. 3
options: A Both statements are correct and statement 1 explains statement 2.
B Both statements are correct but statement 2 does not explain statement 1.
C Statement 1 is correct but statement 2 is incorrect.
D Statement 2 is correct but statement 1 is incorrect. 3

questions.question: Lithium is in Group I of the Periodic Table. Nitrogen is in Group V of the Periodic Table. Lithium reacts with nitrogen to form the ionic compound lithium nitride. What happens to the electrons when lithium atoms and nitrogen atoms form ions? lithium atoms nitrogen atoms A each lithium atom loses one electron to form a Li + ion each nitrogen atom gains three electrons to form an N 3– ion B each lithium atom loses one electron to form a Li + ion each nitrogen atom gains five electrons to form an N 5– ion C each lithium atom gains one electron to form a Li – ion each nitrogen atom loses three electrons to form an N 3+ ion D each lithium atom gains one electron to form a Li – ion each nitrogen atom loses five electrons to form an N 5+ ion 4
questions.text: Lithium is in Group I of the Periodic Table. Nitrogen is in Group V of the Periodic Table. Lithium reacts with nitrogen to form the ionic compound lithium nitride. What happens to the electrons when lithium atoms and nitrogen atoms form ions? lithium atoms nitrogen atoms A each lithium atom loses one electron to form a Li + ion each nitrogen atom gains three electrons to form an N 3– ion B each lithium atom loses one electron to form a Li + ion each nitrogen atom gains five electrons to form an N 5– ion C each lithium atom gains one electron to form a Li – ion each nitrogen atom loses three electrons to form an N 3+ ion D each lithium atom gains one electron to form a Li – ion each nitrogen atom loses five electrons to form an N 5+ ion 4

questions.question: The diagram shows an electrical cable. metal core plastic coating Which statement about the substances used is correct? A The coating is plastic because it conducts electricity well., B The core is copper because it conducts electricity well., C The core is copper because it is cheap and strong., D The core is iron because it is cheap and strong.
questions.text: The diagram shows an electrical cable. metal core plastic coating Which statement about the substances used is correct? A The coating is plastic because it conducts electricity well., B The core is copper because it conducts electricity well., C The core is copper because it is cheap and strong., D The core is iron because it is cheap and strong.
options: A The coating is plastic because it conducts electricity well.
B The core is copper because it conducts electricity well.
C The core is copper because it is cheap and strong.
D The core is iron because it is cheap and strong.

questions.question: The equations below all show redox reactions. Fe 2 O 3 +  3CO  →  2Fe  +  3CO 2 2ZnO  +  C  →  2Zn  +  CO 2 Fe 2 O 3 +  2Al  →  Al 2 O 3 +  2Fe 2CO  +  2NO  →  2CO 2 +  N 2 Which oxide is oxidised in these reactions? A Fe 2 O 3, B CO C ZnO D NO
questions.text: The equations below all show redox reactions. Fe 2 O 3 +  3CO  →  2Fe  +  3CO 2 2ZnO  +  C  →  2Zn  +  CO 2 Fe 2 O 3 +  2Al  →  Al 2 O 3 +  2Fe 2CO  +  2NO  →  2CO 2 +  N 2 Which oxide is oxidised in these reactions? A Fe 2 O 3, B CO C ZnO D NO
options: B CO C ZnO D NO
